methods.method_name: Discrete Fourier Transform and Anomaly Normalization
methods.description: This method involves performing a discrete Fourier transform (DFT) on climate records, padding them with zeros for optimal interpolation, and normalizing the data to anomalies around the mean and standard deviation. This is used to analyze periodicities in climate data.

steps.description: The records were padded with 25,000 zeros, yielding optimum interpolation of the DFT spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: pad records with zeros for DFT interpolation

steps.description: The data were converted to anomalies around the mean and normalized to the SD.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize data to anomalies and standard deviation

methods.method_name: Monte Carlo Simulation for Confidence Curves
methods.description: Monte Carlo simulation is used to generate confidence curves with respect to background noise, allowing for the calculation of confidence levels in the presence of red AR1 noise.

steps.description: Confidence curves with respect to background noise were generated by MC-simulation for which 10,000 pertinent records, in the form of anomalies and normalized to the SD.
steps.searchable_summary: generate confidence curves using Monte Carlo simulation

methods.method_name: Nonlinear Optimization for Sine Function Parameters
methods.description: Nonlinear optimization is used to determine the frequency and phase of sine representations of climate records, minimizing the negative Pearson correlation between the sine function and the original data.

steps.description: The frequency ν and the phases ϕ of the sine representations ∆T sine and ∆P sine were determined by nonlinear optimization (Nelder and Mead, 1965).
steps.searchable_summary: optimize sine function parameters using nonlinear optimization

steps.description: The objective function F (ν, ϕ) to be minimized is the negative Pearson correlation between X = ∆T sine and Y = ∆T (t) resp. X = ∆P sine and Y = ∆P (t).
steps.searchable_summary: minimize negative Pearson correlation for sine function
